Costumed Ilmarinen - Empires and Puzzles Hero Review & Strategy Guide
The blacksmith returns with a Gold Costume that fixes (almost) everything. Massive stats, bigger debuffs, and a Monk class upgrade turn Ilmarinen from zero to hero.
Costumed Ilmarinen Review: The Gilded Hammer Strikes Back
Costumed Ilmarinen: The Gilded Hammer Strikes Back

Just when you thought it was safe to bring your Ice heroes to war, the master smith returns—and this time, he's dipped in gold. Released in July 2025, Costumed Ilmarinen (nicknamed "Gold Costume" or "Toon Ilmarinen" by some, though strictly it's his Gold Kalevala variant) takes everything the original hero did and cranks it up to eleven. With 2026-standard stats that dwarf his original form and an enhanced special skill that punishes enemies even harder, he has reclaimed his spot as a premier support hero for Titans and Rush Wars.

Costumed Ilmarinen trades his rustic blacksmith apron for a more refined, perhaps even "steampunk-lite" aesthetic. He sports goggles (essential for safety when forging stars, presumably) and gear that looks significantly more expensive. The gold accents aren't just for show; they symbolize his upgraded status. While the original art was warm and welcoming, this version looks like he means business—specifically, the business of crushing your defense rating.
This is where the magic happens. Base Ilmarinen had ~756 Attack and ~1472 Health. Costumed Ilmarinen boasts

(before Limit Breaking!). These are true 2026 meta stats. He has nearly double the survivability of his former self. With 1250 Defense, he is an absolute brick wall, capable of surviving multiple hits from modern snipers, giving him the time he desperately needs to charge his Slow mana. He is no longer a liability; he is a tanky powerhouse.
"Forged from Gold" is a direct, brutal upgrade over the original skill:
for others (was 20%). A 50% mana cut is devastating; it essentially resets the enemy's mana bar to half. Even a 30% cut to non-Ice enemies is enough to disrupt timing significantly.

(was -56%). This might seem small, but in damage calculations, every percentage point of defense reduction yields exponential damage returns. This makes him arguably the best Defense Down hero for Green Titans in the game.
Retains the core mechanic of Soul Connection. Linking enemies together while stripping 60% of their defense basically guarantees a team wipe if you follow up with
It's strictly better. Bigger numbers, harder hits, more mana stolen. There is no strategic reason to use the base skill if you have the costume.
Slow. This is still his Achilles' heel. However, with the Monk class (resisting mana slows) and his massive bulk, he is far more likely to fire than before.

With 2451 HP and 1250 Def base, his effective HP is among the highest in the Red element.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The costume retains the excellent family bonus and passive, ensuring he remains durable against his counters.
Taking less damage from Ice specials is even more valuable now that he is likely to be used as a tank or flank in Rush wars. It makes him deceptively hard for blue snipers to kill.
(Heal + Mana on low HP) remains one of the best passives for a slow hero. It effectively shortens his second charge or ensures he gets that one crucial cast off before dying.

(Recovery) fits his high-HP profile perfectly. Since he has a massive health pool, percentage-based regeneration restores a huge amount of HP each turn, making him a nightmare to whittle down in Rush formats or on defense.
Before this costume, Ilmarinen was fading into obscurity, kept alive only by his Titan utility.
The costume catapults him back to the top. His stats compete directly with the "Magic" and "Astral" heroes of late 2025.
He is a top-tier tank or flank. The mana cut plus defense down is often a game-ending combination.
The stats allow him to survive auto-farming in hard stages easily.
Fighter -> Monk. While Fighter (Revive) is annoying,
is generally superior for a slow hero because it allows him to resist ailments that would prevent him from firing (like Silence, Mana Block, or Blind).
The Costume Bonus provides significant boosts to attack, defense, health, and mana generation. This mana bonus helps him break tile breakpoints easier.
The Costume is superior in every way. The Monk class resistance ensures consistency, which is what you need from a slow control hero.
With his massive stats, you have more flexibility, but his role remains Support/Tank.
Makes him nearly unkillable. With over 1300 Defense and 2600+ HP (emblemed), he can tank hits all day. Ideal for War Defense.
Maximizes his high base attack (1151) for tile damage.
Slightly less bulk for PvP, though his base stats are so high it rarely matters.
(Defense/Health) is the way to go. He is already strong enough for Titans; making him survivable for Wars adds more value.
His raw stats are among the best in the game as of early 2026.
-50%/-30% cut buys your team significant time.
Resisting status ailments is crucial for ensuring he fires.
He still requires 12 tiles (or fewer with troops) to fire. This makes him vulnerable to very fast teams or bad boards.
His full mana cut potential (-50%) only applies to Ice enemies, though -30% to others is still respectable.
He can't finish off a low-HP enemy by himself (except with tiles).
A viable tank or flank thanks to his massive bulk and punishing skill.
Great for boss waves where survivability and defense down are key.
Costumed Ilmarinen enables huge damage numbers.
Stacking -Elemental Def, -60% Def (Ilmarinen), and Attack buffs leads to 200k+ tile hits.
As a flank, he punishes anyone bringing Blue heroes to kill the Red/Purple tank. If he fires, the offense is crippled by mana loss and defense down.
Prevents the "Share Damage" from applying to everyone.
Heroes who block ailments prevent the Defense Down.
Do not use them together. Ilmarinen overwrites Wilbur's Defense Down (which is good) but Wilbur overwrites Ilmarinen's with a lower value (bad).

If you are lucky enough to pull this costume, you have one of the best Red support heroes in the game. Max him immediately. Even at Slow speed, his stats allow him to survive in regular raids where older slow heroes would die.

This is the upgrade you were waiting for. If you benched Ilmarinen in 2024, it's time to dust him off. He replaces almost any other Red Defense Down hero for Titans. In Rush Wars, he is a top-tier draft pick. He is absolutely worth the Limit Break materials.
“I got this fella, never had the original so I’ll just wait here patiently... mana reducer? Yes please.”
— A player discovering him for the first time.
“Wonder how he compares with the stat upgrade versus Cinderella.”
— A valid comparison, as both are Red Slayers, but Ilmarinen offers more control.
“I’m going to work on mine next... Big defense down, I think if I can get him to fire and flank him with 2 hard hitters, we could do a lot of damage.”

Costumed Ilmarinen is the perfect example of how a costume can revitalize a hero. He goes from a "niche Titan hero" to a "stat monster with a devastating skill."
